data.title: Fed's Kashkari did not back last rate cut, undecided on December - report
data.description:
Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari did not support the Fed's interest rate cut last month, he told Bloomberg News in an interview, but he's undecided on the next policy move.

"The anecdotal evidence and the data we got just implied to me underlying resilience in economic activity, more than I had expected," he said, noting that this argued for a pause in the Fed's October meeting.

Kashkari said available data since then have suggested "more of the same" for the economy. As for the Fed's meeting next month, he said, "I can make a case depending on how the data goes to cut, I can make a case to hold, and we'll have to see."

To note, Kashkari doesn't hold a vote on the Federal Open Market Committee's rate decisions this year, but he participates in its deliberations.

Some Fed policymakers have also expressed skepticism over the need for a rate cut in December.

Boston Fed President Susan Collins said she'd be hesitant to ease policy further, absent evidence of a notable labor market deterioration. Collins is a voting member in the FOMC this year.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, another FOMC voting member, indicated he was reluctant to cut rates near term due to the lack of inflation data during the government shutdown. "Medium-run, I'm not hawkish on rates. When it's foggy, let's just be a little careful and slow down."

Market participants are currently pricing in 52% odds of a 25-basis point rate cut in December, according to CME's FedWatch tool. A month ago, the odds were around 94%.

data.title: China's BYD to phase out in-house D-Chain payments as supplier pressure mounts
data.description:
China’s electric vehicle giant BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) is shifting away from its in-house payment system called Dilian, which it used to pay suppliers through promissory notes. Instead, the EV maker plans to use commercial paper or bank notes for supplier payments, according to a Reuters report, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The supply chain finance tool called 'D-Chain' was launched in 2018 and allowed the company to keep costs low and speed up production; however, suppliers often faced delays of up to 127 days, above the Chinese industry average of 108 days.

The adjustment is being piloted among certain small and medium-sized suppliers. Due to its broad impact, communication is mostly conducted one-on-one and has not been formally announced, a local Chinese media report pointed out. Many suppliers have not yet received notice, particularly those who halted supply and still hold D-Chain claims.

The shift likely comes amid regulatory pressure to shorten payment times and improve transparency in the auto industry. China’s new rules now require automakers to pay suppliers within 60 days and limit the use of non-cash payments, though loopholes remain.

BYD told Reuters that its Dilian system falls within regulatory guidelines and it has sped up payments to suppliers this year, but it did not address queries about the move away from in-house notes.

BYD Company did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Swiss Re reports nine month ended results; on-track to meet full year guidance
data.description:
• Swiss Re press release (SSREY): reported a net income of USD 4.0 billion and an ROE of 22.5% for the first nine months of 2025, compared with a net income of USD 2.2 billion3 and an ROE of 13.3%4 for the same period in 2024. 

  • The insurance service result, which reflects the underwriting profit earned in the period, was USD 4.8 billion, compared with USD 2.9 billion for the first nine months of 2024.

  • Insurance revenue for the Group amounted to USD 32.0 billion, compared with USD 33.7 billion for the same period in 2024.

  • The Group's new business contractual service margin (CSM), which reflects the profitability of new business written in the period, was USD 3.9 billion, compared with USD 4.2 billion for the prior-year period.

  • Swiss Re's ROI for the first nine months of 2025 was 4.1%, up from 3.9% for the same period in 2024.

  • Outlook

    Swiss Re's Group Chief Executive Officer Andreas Berger said: "Thanks to the strong performance in the first nine months of 2025, we are well on-track to meet our Group net income target of more than USD 4.4 billion for the full year and our combined ratio targets for both of our property and casualty businesses. In L&H Re, we are taking decisive steps to increase resilience. These actions, together with our continued cost discipline, strengthen the core of our business."

data.title: Spain’s inflation rises to 3.1% in October
data.description:
• The inflation rate in Spain increased to 3.1% in October from 3% in September of 2025.
  • Core consumer prices in Spain increased 2.50% in October of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

data.title: France inflation drops to 0.9% in October
data.description:
• The inflation rate in France decreased to 0.90% in October from 1.20% in September of 2025 and is lower than estimates of 1%.
  • On a monthly basis, the Consumer Price Index increased 0.10% in October over the previous month.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Siemens Energy AG GAAP EPS of €0.19, revenue of €10.4B; raises mid-term outlook
data.description:
• Siemens Energy AG press release (SMEGF): Q4 GAAP EPS of €0.19.
  • Revenue of €10.4B (+6.8% Y/Y).
  • Orders amounted to €14.2B, down 2.5% on a comparable basis (excluding currency translation and portfolio effects), primarily because of the high basis of comparison at Siemens Gamesa. Book-to-bill ratio (ratio of orders to revenue) was strong at 1.36. As a result, order backlog rose to a new high of €138B at fiscal year-end.
  • Free cash flow pre-tax largely followed the earnings trend and benefited from timing effects amounting to €1,327m (Q4 FY 2024: €932m).

  • Siemens Energy strives to reach a profit margin before special items in fiscal year 2028 in a range between 14% to 16% (before 10% to 12%). Siemens Energy intends to achieve a compound annual revenue growth on a comparable basis in the low-teens percentage range until fiscal year 2028 (before high single-digit to low double-digit).

  • Fiscal year 2025 results and fiscal year 2026 outlook are in line with expectations. Siemens Energy forecasts comparable revenue growth between 11% and 13% and a profit margin before special items between 9% and 11% in fiscal year 2026.

data.title: Merck seen nearing acquisition of Cidara Therapeutics in deal potentially above $3.3 billion
data.description:
• Merck (MRK) is nearing a deal to buy Cidara Therapeutics (CDTX), a drugmaker pioneering a long-acting antibody medicine that protects against flu, the Financial Times reported.
  • A deal valuing Cidara at a premium to its $3.3 billion market capitalization could be announced as soon as Friday, provided it did not hit any last-minute snags, the report said.
  • Merck was still vying with another pharmaceutical group for the biotech late on Thursday before the seller favoured its offer.
  • The exact price of the deal could not immediately be established. Any deal would likely include cash and the promise of future payments when clinical trial milestones are hit, the FT report said.
  • Cidara (CDTX) jumped more than 40% in post-market trading after the report.
